mentions.note: Stock in freefall with no clear catalyst; hold existing position and wait for CEO Renee Haase interview to clarify SoftBank stake situation and valuation reset.
Very interesting; Renee Huang (CEO) got shortage coming right; great buy. CEO bet validated by chip shortage dynamics.
Competitor position benefiting from data center buildout; stock going parabolic—Cramer doesn't buy parabolas; position being built ahead of May 6 earnings but stock has gotten away.
CPU architecture for AI agents; previously worthless, now gold. Powering new compute economy.
Semiconductor powering AI; part of cohort displacing software as market leader; not specifically recommended.
Pivoting from licensing to manufacturing own CPUs; massive tailwind from AI agents needing advanced processors; CEO Rene Haas bullish and straight with investors.
Up $32 (10%); speaking with Jensen Wong at Computex Monday/Tuesday; Qualcomm, Intel, Marvell also attending; stock me for the charitable trust.
Held up well while other semis stumbled in late-day trading; resilience suggests strength in Computex narrative and chip demand.
RTX Spark Superchip is ARM-based for AI agents; marks Jensen's rejection of Intel/AMD x86 architecture for next generation; stock rallied on announcement.
CEO joke with Nvidia Jensen: every product announcement drives ARM stock higher; unstoppable for past week on AI infrastructure boom.

Huge winner for the charitable trust; sold some at $310 but let it run to $400 without trimming. Lost discipline. Down 12%+ today. Should have exited more aggressively. Lesson: trim parabolic winners even when hypnotized by gains—take 'little schnitle' off table.
Data center compute architecture beneficiary; up 283% YTD; part of endless price target raise cycle on data center growth.
Down 23% over the past year, but Cramer is a buyer, not a seller, citing confidence in CEO Rene Haas.
In every device; hesitate to exit despite weakness at $152. Rene Haas doing terrific job; long-term structural play.

CPU demand explosion from agentic AI, autonomous vehicles, robotics; 22M developer flywheel unbreakable; 50% power efficiency advantage vs. x86; data centers overtaking mobile within years; SoftBank long-only conviction; stock hasn't reflected TAM expansion.
Big beneficiary of CPU-GPU pivot for AI agents; ties tighten with Nvidia as agentic computing requires more flexible processor architectures.
New agentic AI CPU business (targeting $15B+ sales in 5 years) transforms company from licensing-only model; Meta already customer; 2x X86 performance at same power; market cap justified up 25% YTD.
Better than Qualcomm; making series of good steps; recommended over QCOM.
CPU company up 20; put on club buy list days ago; Rene Haas earnings coming in 2 weeks; good business trajectory.
Downgraded from buy to buy-on-weakness after parabolic 34% surge in 4 days; wait for pullback; up 71% since March 30.
Parabolic move threatening to touch sun; correction needed. Owned in trust but stock has run too far; pullback coming.
Designs CPU architecture for most chips; now making CPUs itself—could be romper stock. Up big, then reversal in afternoon; not ideal entry but watch closely.
